name: Materialized Lake View Scheduled Refresh
description: Create an MLV execution definition scoped to specific views, then schedule automated refreshes linked to that definition.
prerequisites: Lakehouse exists with at least one materialized lake view

steps.description: Resolve the lakehouse ID by name if you don't already have it.
Check for existing execution definitions to avoid duplicates.
Create an execution definition that refreshes only the two revenue views in Optimal mode.
Capture the execution definition ID from the previous step's response.
steps.notes: Store the ID in $DEF_ID for the next step.

steps.description: Create a daily (every 1440 minutes) schedule linked to the execution definition.
Trigger an ad-hoc refresh immediately to verify the setup works.

tips: Use mode: All to refresh every MLV in the lakehouse — no selectedMlvs list needed.
Use mode: Selected with selectedMlvs to limit cost and refresh only the views downstream consumers depend on.
A single execution definition can be reused across multiple schedules (e.g., both hourly and on-demand).
To change the refresh scope, use update-execution-definition with a partial JSON body — only provided fields are changed.
Set refreshMode to Full only after major schema changes that invalidate incremental Delta state.
If you have cross-lakehouse MLV lineage, add extendedLineageExecutionContext to the execution definition.
Delete orphan execution definitions with delete-execution-definition after removing the associated schedule.
